Chip Industry Technical Paper Roundup: July 8

Shares

Industry Research

Chip Industry Technical Paper Roundup: July 8

Shares

Schottky barriers at Si/metal interfaces; agentic HLS; probabilistic memory for edge; agentic HW design automation as repository-level code evolution; HW fingerprinting for photonic ICs; functional safety; open-source, customizable RISC-V SoC platform.

July 8th, 2026 -

By: Linda Christensen

New technical papers recently added to Semiconductor Engineering’s library:

Technical Paper
Research Organizations

Effect of Exchange-Correlation Functionals on Schottky Barriers at Si/Metal Interfaces
NIST, University of Maryland, Johns Hopkins University

AgRefactor: Self-Evolving Agentic Workflow for HLS Compatibility and Performance
Carnegie Mellon University, UCLA

Probabilistic Memory for Trustworthy Edge Intelligence
University of Notre Dame, Georgia Tech, and Villanova University

Agentic Hardware Design as Repository-Level Code Evolution
Nvidia Research

Enhancing Co-packaging Optics Enabled Silicon Photonics Security Assurance Hardware Fingerprinting
University of Florida

SafeGen: LLM-Driven Assertion Generation and Fault Criticality Evaluation for Functional Safety
Arizona State University, Texas Instruments India

Croc: Training the Next Generation Chip Designers on Domain-Specific End-to-End Open Source Silicon
ETH Zurich, lowRISC, University of Bologna

 
Find more semiconductor research papers here.

Shares

Tags: Arizona State University Carnegie Mellon University ETH Zurich Georgia Institute of Technology Johns Hopkins University lowRISC National Institute of Standards and Technology NVIDIA Research semiconductor research Texas Instruments India UCLA University of Bologna University of Florida University of Maryland University of Notre Dame Villanova University

Linda Christensen   (all posts)

Linda Christensen is vice president of operations and a contributing writer at Semiconductor Engineering.

Maryland, Johns Hopkins) July 3, 2026 by Technical Paper LinkLLM Agents To Refactor Software For High Level Synthesis (Carnegie Mellon, UCLA) July 3, 2026 by Technical Paper LinkProbabilistic Memory Architecture That Bridges The Gap Between RNG Sampling and Memory Access (Notre Dame, Georgia Tech, Villanova) July 3, 2026 by Technical Paper LinkA Self-Evolving Agent Framework That Treats Hardware Design as Repository-Level Code Evolution (Nvidia Research) June 30, 2026 by Technical Paper LinkHW Fingerprinting Technique for Silicon Photonic ICs Using Photonic Crystal-based Density-Controlled Patterns (U. of Florida) June 29, 2026 by Technical Paper Link
